title: 日本ダービーの後に・疲弊の理由

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text: Huff, huff... Pant, pant... I managed... to make it to the end... But...

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Uuugh... Why... are my legs... so heavy? This feels
like...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Wooooosh!

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
(It feels like I just walked through a fierce
blizzard. Even my toes feel exhausted...)

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text: (So this... is the Japanese Derby...)

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text: Wheeze... huff... huff... Uuugh...

text_block_list.name: Sakura Bakushin O
text_block_list.text:
You okay?! Need a cheer or three?! Here's some
water!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I rush out to meet Kitasan Black, who looks
absolutely crushed with fatigue. Voices behind
me prick my attention.

text_block_list.name: Spectator A
text_block_list.text:
What's up with Kitasan Black? Looks like she ran
into a wall or something...

text_block_list.name: Spectator B
text_block_list.text:
It's probably the distance she ran. Plenty of
Umamusume end up like that after running the
Derby...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
It takes Kitasan Black quite a while to recover
enough to talk normally.

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Yes, I think so... But wow. I was so sure I'd be
tough enough to handle the race.

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Is the Derby just that intense? Or am I this tired
because of something else?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
After some deliberation, I decide to tell her
about the conversation I overheard and am
worrying about.
choice_data_list:
Someone said the race's distance might be the
problem.

Someone said the race's distance might be the
problem.

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Distance... Are you saying 2,400 meters is too
much for me?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I can't say for sure if that's true. Today is the first
time she's run in a G1 race this long.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
As her trainer, I was sure she could handle long
distances, but after watching her run today, I
can't say for certain anymore.

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Hmm, what to do? Maybe shorter races are more
my thing...

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
Up until now, I've always just run all out without
paying much attention to distances and stuff.

text_block_list.name: Sakura Bakushin O
text_block_list.text:
No worries, Kitasan Black! There's nothing to fret
about!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Sakura Bakushin O, who has been listening to us
talk the whole time, inserts herself into the
conversation, full of confidence.

text_block_list.name: Sakura Bakushin O
text_block_list.text:
Today was just a fluke. You're absolutely fine, so
rest assured!

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text: How can you be so sure I'll be okay, Bakushin O?

text_block_list.name: Sakura Bakushin O
text_block_list.text:
Heh heh heh! How could I not be? Just who do
you think your super special coach is, hm?

text_block_list.name: Sakura Bakushin O
text_block_list.text: It is I, Sakura Bakushin O! The Umamusume who has been victorious in three 1,200-meter races—aka 3,600 meters total! That's super long distance!
choice_data_list:
So long distances... are just a couple of short
distances stacked together?!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
As far as I know, Sakura Bakushin O is a
specialized sprinter who has had many great
showings in G1 sprints.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
That said, I've never heard anyone lump three
1,200-meter sprints into one long-distance
3,600-meter race... That's just not sound
reasoning.

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
If Bakushin O says I'll be okay, then I just might
be okay! Right, Trainer?
choice_data_list: Uhh, I'm not really sure it's quite that simple...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I gently try to stop Kitasan Black from taking
Sakura Bakushin O's word at face value.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Distance aptitude aside, there's not much we can
do aside from watch how things unfold on the
track from here on out.

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text: Well, if I'm not really cut out for long races...

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
The autumn Kikuka Sho might be even tougher.
That's 3,000 meters long. I'd better keep my nose
to the grindstone and train real hard!
